intro_quote: “I don’t need a lot… all I need is a top-book.”
intro_lead: This saying best explains my time consuming book procurement, administration and reading process and my disruptive reading technique which enables me to read every free minute and to select only pageturners out of a big variety of books. A dream!!
note_html:
Everything is based on a database which is connected to my physical bookshelf.
More about how and why I did this is explained in About PagesInLine.

home_hero_title: Turn waiting time in reading time.
home_hero_lede: PagesInLine is a paperbook-based method to support a relaxed readingexperience. Pocket sections, real-life minutes, visible progress. No smartphone reading. No e-reader. Just pages.
home_bullet_1: Capture books directly on your smartphone
home_bullet_2: Always know which book you are reading
home_bullet_3: Turn free minutes into reading time faster
home_how_1: Pick a paperback you want to read.
home_how_2: Split it into pocket sections (20–40 pages).
home_how_3: Carry one section and read top-to-bottom.
home_how_4: Use micro-moments (waiting time becomes reading time).
home_how_5: Done pages → recycling (you always know where you stopped).
home_start_lede: Choose one book you actually want. Make one small section. Read every waiting minute today.
footer_disclaimer_text: External links (e.g., YouTube, TikTok, Instagram) lead to third‑party platforms. We are not responsible for their content. Their privacy policies and terms apply.

info.technik.title: How PagesInLine works
info.technik.p1: Swiping on a smartphone means changing content when you’ve seen enough or don’t like it.
info.technik.p2: With paper books that seemed impossible—until I had the idea to cut my books into small pieces. A new world opened up for me.
info.technik.p3: This technique gave me a lot of advantages:
info.technik.p4: Advantage 1: The pages are always close to me when an occasion for reading comes up—supermarket line, check-in, train, plane, and so on. Advantage 2: I can carry several book parts with me and switch if I’m not hooked. Pages I’ve read go to recycling right away, so I always know where I stopped.
info.technik.callout: Use paper books. Keep the overview. Turn spare minutes into reading time.
info.ausruestung.title: My equipment
info.ausruestung.p1: There are some things that are necessary to make my reading dream true.
info.ausruestung.p_markers: Once the cover is removed I still want to know which book I am reading. That’s why I register my books in a Google Sheet and mark them with a barcode. Different colors help me recognize them faster in my bookshelf.
info.ausruestung.p_ducktape: To secure loose pages I use a professional duct-tape dispenser. It’s invaluable to keep all pages in the right place.
info.ausruestung.p_bookshelf: My bookshelf is a central part of my reading strategy. I can see my books with the markings I applied. They are dry and safe. If I want to read one, I just take it out—and if I have free space, I add new ones.
info.ausruestung.p_bag: When traveling I use a reading bag to store my books. It’s better than a backpack because I can easily take books out—and also use it for trash.
info.beschaffung.title: How do I get pageturners?
info.beschaffung.p1: I usually buy my books at flea markets.
info.beschaffung.p2: If I look for new books in a shop, I usually go to Hugendubel in Munich at Karlsplatz. There I can test-read and decide if I buy. Also available online.
info.beschaffung.p3: For used books in Munich I recommend Antiquariat Angelika Ziegler, Landsberger Str. 71, 80339 Munich. Big choice and nice personnel. Also online.
info.faq.title: Questions you might have
info.faq.q1: Why not simply carry the whole book?
info.faq.a1: Because that is often exactly where reading moments fail in daily life. PagesInLine makes paper books easier to use in short free time windows.
info.faq.q2: Why is an app needed for that?
info.faq.a2: Once single pages are on the go, visible information such as title, author or status can disappear. The app therefore keeps track digitally of which book is being read and how far the progress has gone.
info.faq.a3: Because many readers deliberately want to stay with paper books. PagesInLine is for people who want a physical reading experience with a digital overview.
info.faq.a4: Especially for people with many books at home, little free time, and the wish to truly use books in daily life instead of only collecting them.
info.about.title: About PagesInLine
info.about.lede: What drives me to use a reading technique?
info.about.p1: As a kid, I devoured adventure stories—especially Karl May. Later, life got busy and reading slowly slipped away.
info.about.p2: One day I discovered the 3R technique, and it brought books back into my daily routine. Suddenly even small breaks became reading time.
info.about.p3: Because I can move through books efficiently, my “bad habit” of coming home from bookshops and flea markets with a stack of books finally made sense.
info.about.p4: What I love most: the 3R technique helps me find truly good books faster. I can always keep a few options with me—so I’m never stuck without something worth reading.

ao_lede: All authors in my library, grouped alphabetically.
home_title: PagesInLine makes paper books ready for short reading moments on the go — within reach, space-saving, and always available.
home_hero_p1: Keep track of your books and remaining pages with the app.
home_hero_p2: Capture new books in seconds with your smartphone and ISBN scanner. A barcode is generated automatically so each book can always be identified clearly.
home_hero_p3: Even when a book is split into individual pages, the title, author, and reading status stay stored digitally. This lets you mark books as finished, stopped early, or top titles with ease.
home_hero_image_alt: Paper book in a pocket for reading on the go
info.technik.lede: PagesInLine helps prepare paper books so they are ready to read in short free moments — on the go, space-saving, and with a digital overview when needed.
info.technik.side_note: Keep the paper book in everyday use. The app steps in only where identity and progress would otherwise get lost.
info.technik.card1.title: Prepare pages for reading on the go
info.technik.card1.text: Instead of always carrying the whole book, the next pages to read are prepared in advance. That makes the paper book more practical in short free moments.
info.technik.card2.title: Keep each book clearly assigned
info.technik.card2.text: Once title, author or status are no longer visible on the physical part, the app keeps order. ISBN scan and barcode help identify each book clearly.
info.technik.card3.title: Keep reading progress visible
info.technik.card3.text: PagesInLine makes it clear which book is being read, how far the reader has come and where reading continues. That way fewer reading moments are lost.
info.faq.lede: The most important questions about PagesInLine, answered briefly and clearly.
info.faq.side_note: For a first impression, the “How it works” page is usually enough. The FAQ then answers the most common follow-up questions.
beta.title: Beta test for readers who want to use every free moment for reading
beta.lede: PagesInLine is opening first test accesses for people who want to bring paper books into daily life more easily and keep reading progress visible digitally.
beta.status_text: The product core already works. We are now looking for first users to test the flow in real reading routines and improve it in a focused way.
beta.benefit_1: Access to an early version with scanner function and digital book identity
beta.benefit_2: A direct line for feedback from real reading routines
beta.benefit_3: Visibility into the next steps around usage, progress and reading flow
beta.audience_1: People with many books at home
beta.audience_2: Avid readers, commuters and people with little free time
beta.audience_3: Early adopters who want to help shape a concrete reading product
beta.feature_1.title: Capture books directly on a smartphone
beta.feature_1.text: New or existing books can be brought into the system quickly and prepared for daily use right away.
beta.feature_2.title: Keep each book clearly assigned
beta.feature_2.text: Title, author and status stay visible digitally even when the physical book changes in daily use.
beta.feature_3.title: Keep progress visible
beta.feature_3.text: PagesInLine shows what has already been read and where the reader picks up again.
beta.signup_text: Write to us directly by email if you want to try PagesInLine early and share feedback.
